entry.title: Re: SAP data migration
entry.content:
Hi Nehad,

If you are looking for the available objects for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, they are consolidated here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html?version=2502.500 

You may filter for the migration approach "Staging table" or "Direct transfer" depending on your needs as some objects may be available for one approach while not for the other.

For assistance there is also sample data for many migration objects for "File / staging" approach. You may download the prefilled sample XMLs for the objects that are available from the following page https://help.sap.com/docs/DATAMIG_S4HANA/d5aa3caabab04bb68d9cc3352d003330/b8d3c4714d324d3182532a3f6ce63abb.html 

Kind regards,
Zsombor

entry.title: Re: Amount field length extension (AFLE) & Limitations in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Guidogioa!

The workarounds are the ones already provided by SAP that you have detailed above.

Unfortunately, we all believed that S4 would come with 23 digits in every CURR field and in every module, but it is not at all like that. You need to activate AFLE for the 23 digits and only works for FI postings... so useless in complex processes in big companies.

We have similar issues in Venezuela, Colombia, Lebanon, and the approach that is works better for us is by splitting POs or SO and/or invoices, specially if you are using additional local currencies like in USD or EUR. You could also combine all the other options too.

Hope this helps!

Good luck,

Mercedes

entry.title: Empowering Business Users: Unleashing the Power of Key User Extensibility in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
What Is Key User Extensibility?

It is the suite of browser‑based tools built into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that lets designated “key users” personalize and extend standard applications, data structures, and business rules—completely code‑free. All extensions are upgrade‑safe, maintainable, and governed by role‑based authorizations.

Simplifying the Business User Experience

UI Adaptation at Runtime

Key users enter Adaptation mode directly within the Fiori launchpad to hide, rename, reposition, or add fields and UI elements on standard apps—no ABAP or UI5 coding required. Once saved, changes persist through quarterly upgrades, keeping the interface aligned with user needs.

Custom Fields & Logic

The Custom Fields app allows users to introduce new fields (for example, adding “StockOwner” to the Warehouse Stock report), then publish these across UIs, reports, analytics, and APIs instantly. In parallel, the Custom Logic app (powered by BRFplus) lets users define simple rules—defaults, validations, derivations—without writing code.

Custom Business Objects

When unique data entities are needed—such as “CycleCountSchedule” for non‑standard count plans—key users define Custom Business Objects. The tool automatically generates database tables, OData services, Fiori list/object pages, and API endpoints, making custom masters behave like standard ones.

In‑App Business Rules Framework

For conditional logic or simple automations (e.g., “If |PostedQty – PickedQty| > 5, set TransferOrderStatus to ‘Review’”), the in‑app BRFplus interface enables rule creation, testing, and assignment within the Custom Logic app—keeping business logic close to the process.

Use Cases (Warehouse‑Focused Scenarios)

I am providing below warehouse specific use cases but similar use cases can be extended to other LoBs as well.

  1. Temperature‑Sensitive Handling Units
    A cold‑chain operator adds a “Temperature Range” field to the Handling Unit context, making it visible in packing UIs and IoT integrations for real‑time monitoring.
  2. Tailored Warehouse Monitor Views
    The control‑tower team hides unnecessary columns and reorders critical fields (SKU, Available Qty, Storage Type, Quality Status) via UI Adaptation, then saves a “Quality Check” variant—streamlining exception handling.
  3. Custom Cycle Count Schedule Object
    Inventory leads create a CBO named “CycleCountSchedule,” automatically generating a Fiori app and APIs that mobile workers use to pull active count plans and report results via handheld scanners.
  4. Auto‑Block Transfers on Quantity Mismatch
    A rule in BRFplus blocks any transfer order when the posted vs. picked quantity delta exceeds five units, flagging it for review and preventing inventory inconsistencies.
  5. Enhanced Warehouse‑Stock Report
    The team adds “StockOwner” via Custom Fields and includes it in the embedded analytics query—enabling stock‑by‑owner analysis (in‑house vs. 3PL) directly in Fiori dashboards.

Governance & Security

All extensibility tools are controlled by role‑based authorizations, ensuring only users assigned the Key User Extensibility role can access Adapt UI, Custom Fields, Custom Business Objects, or Custom Logic apps—maintaining strict change management and audit trails.

Upgrade Safety & Integration

SAP’s cloud‑native extensibility framework preserves all in‑app modifications across quarterly releases, eliminating upgrade rework. Standard SAP code remains untouched through a clear separation of core and extensions via published APIs and extension points.

Business Impact

  • Faster Time‑to‑Market: Key users implement changes in days, not weeks.
  • Reduced IT Backlog: Frees developers for strategic initiatives.
  • Higher User Adoption & Satisfaction: Personalized UIs and processes.
  • Continuous Innovation: Rapid experimentation without technical debt.

Who Benefits?

  • Business Users craving tailored experiences
  • Consultants empowering clients for self‑sufficiency
  • Decision‑Makers driving agility and cost efficiency

Next Steps

  1. Open the Custom Fields app to add a sample field.
  2. Launch Adapt UI in your Fiori Launchpad and save a variant.
  3. Explore Custom Business Objects in the Extensibility Library.

SAP Help Portal Links

  • Adapt UI at Runtime: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4fc8d03390c342da8a60f8ee387bca1a/5c424437bf794f809087fdce391149f2.html
  • Custom Fields & Logic: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/57909455bf7c4fdd8bcf48d76c1eae33.html
  • Creating Custom Fields: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3a713139e548434e8a50800e7a7c8172.html
  • Custom Business Objects: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b45696ca0d9143cba040797e9c71aa44.html
  • Create Custom Business Object (Extensibility Explorer): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENSIBILITY_EXPLORER/b0e8d558ba2f47f5b02a3fc0ac9edc34/6bd534db33694eba9ddde788ef717dfd.html
  • Business Rule Framework plus: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/8b78ae42ec1c4b75937806fcd4fd7d1c/9a6b67ce7c26446483af079719edf679.html
  • Enabling UI Adaptation at Runtime (Admin): https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_BW4HANA/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/402b392585834dd38dd381d8f04b2616.html
  • Adapting the UI of an SAP Fiori App at Runtime: https://help.sap.com/docs/UI_ADD-ON_FOR_SAP_NETWEAVER_20/17ae0e97e0fc424a9c368f350c0ba6bd/5c424437bf794f809087fdce391149f2.html
  • Custom Logic App: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3aa57e5b9a9f46218ef8cc38d703c8f4.html
  • Custom Fields & Logic App (NetWeaver): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_AS_ABAP_751_IP/b4367b1cec3243c4989f0ff3d727c4ab/57909455bf7c4fdd8bcf48d76c1eae33.html
  • BRFplus Enhancements: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/2fa59f4a0ec249b0aa07aafc7c1ea4c4.html

entry.title: Nominal -/Original Value in Credit-Risk Based Impairment
entry.content:
Dear All, 

when executing the run for "Post Credit-Risk Based Impairment" the result list shows for e.g. as nominal -/original value 541,90€

However the line item report and hence the posting document shows a different value.

When trying to reconcile the difference between amounts shown in the result list vs. the posting document like net amount, discounts and so on there is no way to explain the amount shown as Nominal -/Original Value in Credit-Risk Based Impairment. The differene only occurs with accounts subject to VAT taxation, nevertheless it is not possible to match the amounts given in the result list

Does anyone know how the system is determining the amount?

entry.title: Re: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Xavier /@xjhacking 

When we embedded SAC back in 2019 into S/4HANA Cloud the intend was not to only focus on SAC Stories. Each 'analytical tool' has its benefits depending on the use case and eSAC Stories (so far) would not be able to replace them. Some of the above listed is historically grown and heavily used by our customers.

But yes, you are right telling us your concerns. Still, looking at the new innovations with MDA or Review Booklet, we achieve improved consistency across our analytic capabilities by reusing the same controls and access libraries in Fiori/UI5 as we use with SAC.

entry.title: Re: Automatically Generate Packaging Items in EWM
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog entry. 
What about a scenario where you need to store the packaging materials in the rack and then do a replenishment to the GM bin?

 

e.g. 3 pallets of packaging material car6 with 270pc p.pal. arrive in the warehouse and have to be stored in the rack until needed. one day, the GM bin runs low and needs REPL, can packaging materials be stored in rack and then REPL (automatically or manually) to the GM bin so that they can be used and postet GI?

At the moment the solution would be to create a dummy material for each packaging material so that this can be stored and REPL and then scrapped while the whole stock of the original packaging material is on the GM bin, but that seems unnessecarily complicated and prone to errors with stock keeping.

entry.title: BAPI_COSTCENTER_GETLIST1 how parameters *FROM and *TO work?
entry.content:
The function module BAPI_COSTCENTER_GETLIST1 has several parameters doubled, one with suffix FROM, one with suffix TO, e.g. COSTCENTER_FROM and COSTCENTER_TO. I understand that they are intervals but isn't it possible to select all values easily or one exact value or all values starting with a constant literal?

The doubled parameters suffixed FROM and TO (instead of * below) are:

  • COSTCENTER_*
  • COMPANYCODE_*
  • PERSON_IN_CHARGE_*
  • DATE_*
  • BUSINESS_AREA_*
  • PERSON_IN_CHARGE_USER_*

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: BAPI_COSTCENTER_GETLIST1 how parameters *FROM and *TO...
entry.content:
All the parameters except DATE_* work the same:

FROMTO# of lines in the range (zero =select all values)SIGNOPTIONLOWHIGH
EmptyEmptyZeroIN/AN/AN/A
Not emptyNot emptyOneIBTFROMTO
Contains *EmptyOneICPFROM 
Not empty and not contains *EmptyOneIEQFROM 
EmptyNot emptyOneILETO 

The logic for all 5 parameters can be found in the subroutine BAPI_FILL_SELECTION_TABLE of the function group KMA1, and the logic for initializing the range can be found in the subroutine BCF_APPEND_COSEL_LINE.

Concerning the two parameters DATE_FROM and DATE_TO, they work as an interval except if they are left empty, it's the interval from 19000101 to 99991231.

entry.title: Check Horizon Zero Confirmation and BPS scheduling with Working Times getting unconfirmed quantities
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently facing an issue with sales orders not getting confirmed when Scope of aATP is defined with Check Horizon - Zero Confirmation (see below) and BPS is active for scheduling with Working Times assigned to specific activities (Load).

Although Scheduling is working as expected and considering the activities that have been defined and attributes durations and working times when maintained, I am facing an issue with Sales Orders quantities not getting confirmed.

1. BPS is active and activity Load has been set up with durations and working time

2. Activity Pick maintained only with durations

3. There is plenty of stock available for this material

4. Check horizon is 14 days (end lead time: 02.06.2025). So anything before that date should be confirmed with current stocks. However, see below for the ATP screen (no quantity confirmed)

This seems to be somehow affected by the Workime Time assigned to activity load for the characteristic combination assigned. 

The working time was defined with shifts on Mondays,  Tuesdays and Wednesdays of 1 minute, meaning in this case that GI can only happen at 18h00 on these 3 days.

From a Scheduling perspective, everything works perfectly fine (as you can see above). However, Availability Check is not confirming quantities with the Zero Confirmation Scope Horizon setup as it seems to get in conflict with the working times assigned in scheduling for later activities. However, this should not be relevant as Material Availability Date is always the date taken into account to determine availability.

Any idea or can someone please provide some inputs? 

Thanks a lot!!!

entry.title: Re: invalid path for report file
entry.content:
Hi,

when this happens next time please see if SAP Note 3151730 relates to your case and you have 74 files in temp folder.

3151730 - Invalid Report File Path with Crystal Reports
Component: SBO-REP-CR (SAP Business One > Reporting > SAP Crystal Reports for SAP Business One), Version: 1, Released On: 29.04.2022
SAP Business One Notes
Symptom
Invalid Report File Path error appears when you preview or print a certain amount of Crystal reports from SAP Business One.
Reproducing the issue
Prerequisites:
You are using SAP Business One 10.0 FP2111 and are printing documents with Crystal Reports.
Scenario:
1.
Go to Sales A/R → Document Printing.
2.
Choose any document type and choose OK. Make sure you have more than 74 records in the Document Printing list.
3.
Choose at least 75 documents and choose Print.
Expected behavior:
All documents are printed successfully.
Actual behavior:
74 documents are printed successfully and the remaining documents are not executed successfully with error returned: Invalid Report File Path

 

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: Quering document lines in user define values
entry.content:
Hi,

try using this FMS in an nvarchar field and see if it populates correctly. If yes, that means your result is coming back in a different type than expected, so most likely you need to cast it as integer or use substitute to change (usually its problems with comma(,) and dot(.) being misinterpreted).

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Request for Order Settlement Based on GL Account to Different WBS Elements
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We would like to inquire if it's possible to configure an order settlement rule that allows cost settlement based on General Ledger (GL) accounts to different WBS elements. Our business requirement is as follows:

  • Diesel costs (e.g., posted to a specific GL account) should be settled to WBS Element A (e.g., for fuel-related costs).

  • Spare parts costs (posted to another GL account) should be settled to WBS Element B (e.g., for material/repair costs).

In essence, we need to split the settlement of an order based on the cost origin (GL account) so that each type of cost is settled to the correct corresponding WBS element.

We request your guidance on whether this can be achieved via standard SAP functionality, such as:

  • Creating multiple settlement rules with account assignment filters (GL-based)?

  • Using source structures and assignment rules in settlement profiles?

  • Any enhancement/BAdI recommendations, if standard functionality does not support this?

Your assistance on how best to approach and configure this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2505?
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is an SaaS solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). Companies can use it to kickstart a master data management initiative at low entry barriers. For details, see also the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition overview and deep-dive presentations.

What's New?

Continuing the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2505. New features come in the areas of data quality management and SOAP replication of business partner.​

Data Quality Management

New features in the Validation Rules for Business Partners app​ include:

  • Delete the validation rules' dependencies to other objects (for example, simulations, dimensions, or data quality evaluation runs) when deleting rules​
  • Disable several validation rules at once​
  • Filter for enabled usages, disabled usages and usages in general​

SOAP Replication

The enhanced SOAP replication of business partners includes:

  • ​Replication of business partners with their relationships​
  • Selective replication of specific business partner roles​
  • Automatic retriggering of business partner relationship messages

Workflow Configuration

Note: As of release 2505, you must configure a mail destination to send notifications. To do this, create a mail destination in your subaccount, as described in Configure Workflow Capability Mail Destination.

For a complete overview of all updates, see the latest What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition.

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

 

 

Best,

Markus

​

entry.title: SAP Conference for Energy and Utilities 2025: A Powerful Gathering in Rotterdam
entry.content:
Hoi SAP Energy and Utilities Community,

What a conference it was! From April 8–10, the vibrant city of Rotterdam played host to the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025, presented by TAC Insights  — and it truly lived up to a memorable excitement. With a packed agenda, inspiring speakers, and a buzzing exhibition floor, this event brought together the best minds and innovations in the energy and utilities sector.

To give you a glimpse of our conference, we've put together a short video for you

 

The event kicked off with our Pre-Conference Workshop Day, offering attendees a chance to explore key industry topics in depth. Sessions included Rise and Grow with SAP, The Future of Energy Supply and Distributed Energy Resources, Enhancing Customer Experience in Utilities, SAP EAM & Performance Management Insights, and SAP for Oil, Gas, and Energy.

In the afternoon, we hosted a series of drop-in sessions covering cutting-edge themes such as AI, Sustainability, Business Transformation Management, SAP Business Network, and Service Management.

In parallel, more than 50 industry leaders came together at the Executive Digital Exchange for vibrant discussions on the most critical trends shaping the future of the Energy and Utilities sector.

In the evening Capgemini sponsored a get-together with welcome drinks. 

Inspiring Keynotes and Panels

On April 8, the main conference started and over 750 attendees explored the latest in renewable energy, digital transformation, and sustainability. The sessions sparked meaningful conversations about how technology is shaping the future of energy and utilities.

From the opening keynote by Daniela Haldy-Sellmann, Global VP and Head of Energy & Utilities Industries at SAP, to the unforgettable talk by Ann Daniels, polar explorer and record-holder — the stage was filled with powerful voices urging us to embrace change and lead with purpose.

Break-Out Track Sessions

Each of the seven dynamic tracks—AI, Asset Management, Customer Experience, Distributed Energy Resources, Water, Utilities, and Oil, Gas, and Energy—kicked off with a compelling SAP presentation, setting the stage for engaging sessions where customers and partners shared real-world insights and success stories.

Our Microforums 

In microforum sessions, the energy and utilities community had the opportunity to get a brief overview on topics such as SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, SAP Business Data Cloud, and initiatives like Green Hydrogen and the SAP Energy Park. Attendees explored the SAP Green Ledger and SAP Field Logistics, gaining valuable insights into sustainability and operational efficiency. The integration of AI within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) was a key focus, showcasing its transformative impact. Additionally, innovations in customer experience (CX) were highlighted, emphasizing their growing importance in today’s dynamic market landscape.

Exhibition & SAP Pop-Up Campus

The SAP booth drew huge interest, once again becoming a vibrant hub of activity. Attendees eagerly explored  live solution demos and engaged directly with SAP industry experts. From personalized advice to in-depth discussions, the booth offered hands-on insights and was a place again where customers and partners could see SAP technology in action. 

Partner Exhibition:

We were proud to collaborate with 44 exceptional partners who played a key role in making this year’s event a success. Alongside their customers, they contributed valuable insights by presenting in track sessions, sharing real-world use cases and innovative industry solutions. Their active presence in the exhibition hall and microforums fostered meaningful discussions and hands-on engagement, creating a dynamic space for knowledge exchange and collaboration.

 

New Highlight: SAP Pop-Up Campus

The SAP Pop-up Campus was a highlight of this year’s event, offering an immersive experience with massive 180° screens that vividly brought the future to life. Attendees explored the transformative potential of AI in SAP solutions for utilities, gaining insights into real-world applications. Sessions on SAP Customer Experience showcased innovative approaches to building customer-centric services. A creative session titled "A Day in the Life of an Electron or Water Drop" illustrated the value of asset management across lifecycle stages. Finally, a forward-looking presentation on S.ENERGY in 2028 revealed how the company plans to serve customers following a successful SAP S/4HANA Utilities and DER platform implementation.

 

Networking & Celebration

At the SAP Conference for Energy and Utilities, networking proved to be one of the most valuable aspects—bringing together professionals from across the industry to exchange ideas, spark collaborations, and build lasting relationships that extend far beyond the event itself.

Business Women Networking Lunch

Over 70 women and men joined our well-established luncheon, featuring the inspiring voices of Ann Daniels and Sarah Zeljko from Energy Queensland. This special session created a welcoming space for open dialogue where attendees shared aspirations, tackled industry challenges, and exchanged innovative ideas. It was a powerful reminder of the importance of connection, collaboration, and the impact of remarkable women leading the way in energy and utilities.

Evening Boat Cruise

On the first day of the conference, attendees came together for an unforgettable evening aboard the OceanDiva in Rotterdam. As the ship glided through the water, everyone celebrated together, enjoying fantastic music from a live DJ, delicious food, and vibrant entertainment—all set against the stunning backdrop of the city skyline.

Personal Highlights & Key Take Aways

1. RISE & GROW with SAP: Utilities are accelerating digital transformation with SAP’s tailored cloud journeys—RISE for end-to-end transformation and GROW for fast, scalable innovation.

2. SAP Business AI: AI is powering smarter utilities—enhancing forecasting, automating operations, and personalizing customer experiences with real-time intelligence.

3. New Business with Distributed Energy Resources: DER are opening new revenue streams—SAP is enabling utilities to manage, monetize, and optimize decentralized energy models.

4. Diversity on Stage: Representation matters—diverse voices and inclusive leadership took the spotlight, driving richer dialogue and more innovative outcomes.

5. Strength of the Community: The SAP ecosystem is thriving—collaboration, co-innovation, and shared success stories are fueling a united energy transition.

Stay Connected

The event may be over, but let’s stay connected until we meet again in 2026—sign up for our newsletter to stay in the loop with future events, insights, and innovation, and be the first to hear where we’ll gather next year! 

A heartfelt thank you to everyone who made this event truly special once again—especially our incredible customers, partners, colleagues, TAC Insights team, and inspiring speakers, whose contributions brought insight, innovation, and energy to every moment!

As a side note, we are proud to announce that our SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference (Seville 2024) has been shortlisted as a finalist in the 'Best Overseas Conference/Event (up to 1,400 attendees)' category at the prestigious Conference & Events Awards 2025, hosted by Awards International. ☀️

entry.title: Re: Request for Order Settlement Based on GL Account to Different WBS Elements
entry.content: Hi Mohit, this is a common scenario where cost settlement needs to be granular based on GL accounts. Standard SAP does support creating multiple settlement rules with account assignment filters, which can help direct costs to different WBS elements based on GL accounts. Using settlement profiles with source structures and assignment rules is definitely the way to go. If you find standard functionality limiting, exploring BAdI enhancements like BADI_SD_SALES_ORDER_SAVE or BADI_SETTLEMENT might help customize the logic further. By the way, for streamlining communication and task management around such configurations, tools like Phonexa can help automate follow-ups and internal notifications to keep your team coordinated efficiently. Hope this helps!

entry.title: HANA FUNCTION LOCATE WRONG POSITION RESULT
entry.content:
Hello guys,
I'm in trouble with LOCATE function returning data on HANA Database (HANA v. 2.00.059.09...).

This is the query a run:
SELECT T0.U_ALD_VARIANTI
,LOCATE(T0.U_ALD_VARIANTI,'=') AS POS
,LOCATE(T0.U_ALD_VARIANTI,'=',-1) AS POSX
,LOCATE(T0.U_ALD_VARIANTI,'=',-2) AS POSY
FROM OITM T0 WHERE t0."ItemCode"='DISCOFERRO2A$CL.1_Fg.II75DIAM60'

And the scope is to find position of the first and second char '=' in the string 'CLRIF=CL.1;CLFIG=_Fg.II75;TGSEG=DIAM60'.
The positions are 6, 17 and 32 but as you can see in the image below, for second '=' in position 17 the function doesn't return me the correct position.

I believe that may be because of the underscore ( '_' ) in the string; probably LOCATE function use a search LIKE in the string.

Please, have you any suggestion or there is another function instead of LOCATE for obtaining the same find result?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: CL_DD_DOCUMENT Refresh Issue
entry.content:
This issue can be fixed in 2 steps:

1. You have to make sure that the object reference declared must be global. This is to ensure when you try to clear/refresh the object reference before next use, it is available to do so. If you keep the object reference as local, the program had no way of accessing previous container and you won't be able to clear it before next use.

data: go_document type ref to cl_dd_document. (global field, preferably in TOP include)

IF go_document IS BOUND.
  go_document->initialize_document( ).
ELSE.
  CREATE OBJECT go_document.
ENDIF.

2. Once you have added the text, make sure to use reuse_control parameter as 'X' in the display document method (as shown below)

go_document->display_document(
  EXPORTING
     reuse_control = 'X'
     container = 'CONTAINER_NAME' " Name of Container (New Container Object Generated)
  EXCEPTIONS
     html_display_error = 1 
     OTHERS = 2
).

entry.title: Email Notifications: Token for Employee Title
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We have a requirement to include an employee’s title (e.g., Dr.) before the name in some email notifications: Document Routing Notification, Document Completed Notification, Personal Compensation Statement Generation notification

I’ve searched through the available SAP token lists but couldn’t find any token related to title. Has anyone worked with this before, or is aware if such a token exists?

I assume this might be a usual common requirement, so I’m hoping there’s a way to retrieve it. 

Any suggestions or guidance would be much appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

Best regards,

Are you able to support us in either changing the versioning to "date" or deleting all consents?

 

Thank you for your support.

 

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Consents versioned by number instead of date
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

There is no option to change versioning mode for existing consents.

You can delete the consent via console or accounts.deleteConsentStatement.
Then you create the consent with versioning by date.

Please refer Product documentation on the following link below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CUSTOMER_DATA_CLOUD/8b8d6fffe113457094a17701f63e3d6a/2782415067b047b7bf10a1439d3e67ce.html?locale=en-US

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Automate tasks in FO in S/4 HANA Public cloud TM
entry.content:
Hello Freinds,

we are in explore phase of our project for S/4 HANA CLoud TM. I have given demo to client on scope item 6W2, 6W3 and 6W4. CLient says that it is too much of manual work to do in FO for e.g one has to do manual activity "setting to ready for Transport execution " and also create "delivery manually by clicking "create delivery button" and also manually add events like check in, check out etc and also manually enter procuring company code, purchase org.

How can we automate the above. I could not find standard batch jobs for delivery creation and "ready for Transp execution status". Can someone point out if the batch job exists ?

ANy pointers how check in check out and purchase org etc can be automated without users to manually  enter them ?

iam of the view S/4 HANA cloud is for those customers who agree on fit to standard but  in our case, things are but different.

rgds

sar

entry.title: Re: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
Hi @Frank703,

first of all, thanks for the remark.

For the official note, we cannot speak, so maybe you reach out to the well known owners (e.g. @OlgaDolinskaja or @ThFiedler ).

But I have another idea on this: You should be able to use this check through the remote check architecture. We have not yet tested this in real life, but the checks we are inheriting from are all remote enabled. So if you have a 2023 (e.g. Sandbox) in your landscape, Kernseife Check should be working remotely as well.

Let's get in direct contact and give it a try!

entry.title: Personal Insights: Integrating CRISP-DM and TOGAF: A Unified Approach for Enterprise AI Architecture
entry.content:
After six years working as a data scientist on custom AI use cases, I’ve come to appreciate the power of structured frameworks like the CRISP-DM (Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining). Its iterative phases - business understanding, data understanding & preparation, modeling, evaluation, and deployment - have consistently helped me to turn complex AI use cases into real, measurable results. Now, as I transitioned into enterprise architecture, I’m discovering how another framework, TOGAF (The Open Group Architecture Framework), brings a new layer of clarity. It offers a strategic lens that complements AI development by aligning it with broader business goals, making integration and deployment of AI more sustainable and scalable.

To me, TOGAF and CRISP-DM aren’t separate or competing methodologies—they’re complementary. When used together, they create a powerful synergy that bridges strategic business alignment with hands-on AI development. This integrated approach is especially valuable when designing AI architectures that need to stay aligned with business objectives yet remain adaptable to evolving business & technical requirements.

In this blog, I will share some concrete personal insights aimed at helping data scientists and enterprise architects better understand each other - so they can more effectively bring AI solutions into production.

Let’s start with the CRISP-DM shown in the following figure.

 

 

 

The CRISP-DM is a structured framework for developing data science projects. It consists of six iterative phases:

  1. Business Understanding – Define project goals and business objectives.
  2. Data Understanding – Collect and explore data to gain insights.
  3. Data Preparation – Clean and transform data for modeling.
  4. Modeling – Apply AI algorithms and techniques.
  5. Evaluation – Assess model performance against business goals.
  6. Deployment – Implement and monitor the solution.

The process is iterative, allowing teams to refine their approach as they learn more through each phase. It's widely used because it's flexible, industry-agnostic, and focused on delivering actionable results.

TOGAF is a framework for designing, planning, and governing enterprise IT architecture. It helps align technology with business goals through a structured, phased approach called the Architecture Development Method (ADM).

 


The ADM is the core of TOGAF—a step-by-step process for developing and managing enterprise architecture. It guides projects through phases like vision, business, data, application, and technology architecture, ensuring alignment with business goals and evolving requirements.

Let’s look at three concrete examples, how these methodologies relate to each other:

  1. Business Understanding – Business Architecture

 

 

 

 

A clear understanding of the business requirements a data science project aims to solve is critical for its success - and this begins with a strong business architecture. Defining the scope means asking key questions: Which business units are involved? What KPIs are we trying to improve? What processes are currently in place to achieve those metrics? These considerations shape essential deliverables like functional and non-functional requirements, a stakeholder map, and process documentations. Tools like SAP LeanIX or SAP Signavio can support this phase by providing visibility into the current business landscape and aligning initiatives with strategic goals.

  1. Data Preparation – Data Architecture, Application Architecture, Opportunities and Solutions & Migration Planning

 

 

Data preparation goes beyond cleaning and transforming datasets - it’s deeply connected to both data and application architecture. This phase involves evaluating the quality and availability of data across IT systems and deciding on the best approach to ensure its fit for modeling. For example, should we build ongoing data wrangling pipelines, or address the issues directly at the source? Which stakeholders are responsible for cleaning the data? Business or IT? These decisions impact not only the project's technical setup but also its long-term sustainability. Key deliverables include for example a baseline architecture consisting of relevant systems & actors and a solid requirement management plan. SAP solutions like SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP HANA Cloud play a vital role in supporting these efforts at scale.

  1. Modelling – Technology Architecture, Opportunities & Solutions, Implementation Governance

 

 

The modeling phase sits at the intersection of technology architecture, opportunities and solutions, and implementation governance. A key decision here is whether to use a pre-built, integrated models or develop a custom AI solution. This involves evaluating model performance, assessing technical feasibility, and identifying infrastructure requirements for development. These considerations shape the overall technology stack and integration strategy. A clear data flow diagram is a crucial deliverable, helping to visualize how data moves through the systems. SAP solutions like Joule, Embedded AI and the AI Foundation provide robust support for both pre-integrated and custom AI modeling approaches.

As we continue to integrate AI into enterprise ecosystems, aligning the business strategy with the AI target architecture is more important than ever. Frameworks like CRISP-DM and TOGAF offer valuable structures to ensure that AI projects are not only technically sound but also strategically aligned. Whether it’s through defining clear problem statements, preparing quality data, or choosing the right modeling approach, each phase plays a crucial role in delivering successful AI solution concepts. By bridging the worlds of data science and enterprise architecture, we can create AI-driven systems that deliver real value, scalability, and long-term impact.

If you are curious to explore more, have a look at the following references:

Enterprise AI Practice — Responsibilities and Deliverables

Data Science and​ Architecture: Building bridges with CRISP-DM and TOGAF

CRISP-DM: Towards a Standard Process Model for Data Mining

I want to thank Stefan Fassmann and Johannes Euler for their support and insights while crafting this blog post.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Fri 06 Jun in Wrocław, PL at the WSB Merito University (Building G, Fabryczna 14G, 2nd floor, room 220). The event is kindly being organized by Capgemini Poland, thank you! The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams.

entry.title: Re: SPED output type error
entry.content:
Hello @GOGUL 

VL02N error is not a real error. That is how SPED is processed. The output processing is delegated to a separate LUW. This way SPED can avoid lock conflicts during immediate processing. 

The real problem shows in SM58. Neither /SPE/CALL_PROC_NAST nor standard SPED processing call screens. You must have some custom logic implemented that calls a screen during RFC processing, which is forbidden. Check ST22 dumps for more details.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: MDG spae parts dictionary
entry.content:
The title should be MDG spare parts dictionary.

In 2009, a global, quality controlled SAP noun-modifier material list was created; It is a:
* Universally Standardized List
* An up-to-date QMS Controlled Document
* A structure that eliminates search redundancies

SAP NOUN-MODIFIER LIST
Noun-Modifier Structure

Noun: SAP Descriptions begin with using the noun. For example – the material master for a ball valve would be described as VALVE-BALL.

Modifier: The modifier is used in conjunction with a noun to identify the item more precisely. For example – the noun modifier description for a panel enclosure would be ENCL-PNL.

Attribute: Used to describe a noun in more detail.

For example – the first attribute to help describe enclosures (ENCL) is size. Therefore the naming convention of a panel enclosure with a size of mm by 1150 mm is ENCL-PNL,1450x1150.

Using the Materials List – Menu Tab

SAP Noun with a modifier:
Noun-Modifier,Attribute1,Attribute2, etc.
Example: TANK-CIP,3800L,HDPE,SS STAND

SAP Noun without a modifier:
Noun,Attribute1,Attribute2,Attribute3 etc.
EXAMPLE: PIPE,PVC,PN10,SDR21,D225,GF

Not all nouns will have a modifier associated.
A dash “-” immediately following the noun indicates a modifier.

Source: http://slideplayer.com/slide/10593564/

Alternatively you can also use the Petroleum Industry Data Dictionary (PIDD) given by Petroleum Industry Data Exchange (PIDX) template for List of commonly used Noun, Modifier & Attributes in Petroleum Industry from below link.

https://pidx.org/pidx-data-request-form/

References:

https://pidx.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Mastering-Data-Management-and-Code-Lists-with-PIDX-Chris-Welsh-PIDX.pdf

#MasterDataManagement #MasterDataGovernance #MaterialMasterStandardization #MaterialMasterNomeclatureStandardization #MaterialMasterTaxonomy #SAPMDM #SAPMDG

entry.title: Export Delivery Header Data (LIKP) to Application Server(AL11) as CSV File
entry.content:
" Table declaration for delivery header
TABLES: LIKP.

" Selection screen for delivery number range and output file path
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
  SELECT-OPTIONS : S_VBELN FOR LIKP-VBELN.      " Delivery numbers
  PARAMETERS     : P_FILE   TYPE CHAR120.       " Directory path on application server
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK B1.

" F4 Help to select file path from the application server (AL11)
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR P_FILE.
  CALL FUNCTION '/SAPDMC/LSM_F4_SERVER_FILE'
    EXPORTING
      DIRECTORY        = SPACE                  " Optional: Can be restricted to a specific directory
      FILEMASK         = SPACE                  " Optional: Restrict file types if needed
    IMPORTING
      SERVERFILE       = P_FILE                 " Selected file path
    EXCEPTIONS
      CANCELED_BY_USER = 1
      OTHERS           = 2.

  " Local variable to store the selected physical path (not used further here)
  DATA(G_PHYSICAL_PATH) = P_FILE.

" Start of the main program logic
START-OF-SELECTION.

  " Fetch delivery header data for selected VBELNs
  SELECT VBELN, ERNAM, ERZET, VKORG, VSTEL
    FROM LIKP
    INTO TABLE @DATA(LT_LIKP)
    WHERE VBELN IN @S_VBELN.

  " Convert the internal table to CSV format using standard SAP function
  DATA: LT_CSV TYPE TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
    EXPORTING
      I_FIELD_SEPERATOR    = ';'                " Use semicolon as delimiter
*     I_LINE_HEADER        =                    " Optional: Add header line
*     I_FILENAME           =                    " Not needed here
*     I_APPL_KEEP          = ' '                " Not relevant for this use case
    TABLES
      I_TAB_SAP_DATA       = LT_LIKP            " Data to be converted
    CHANGING
      I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = LT_CSV             " Output table in CSV format
    EXCEPTIONS
      CONVERSION_FAILED    = 1
      OTHERS               = 2.

  IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    " TODO: Add error message handling
  ENDIF.

  " Concatenate the final file name with directory path and date
  CONCATENATE P_FILE '\Likp_' SY-DATUM INTO DATA(P1_FILE).

  " Open file for writing on the application server in text mode
  OPEN DATASET P1_FILE FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.

  " Write CSV data line-by-line to the file
  LOOP AT LT_CSV INTO DATA(LS_CSV).
    TRANSFER LS_CSV TO P1_FILE.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Close the file after writing
  CLOSE DATASET P1_FILE.

entry.title: Re: How do I select which ID origin personalizes a conten...
entry.content:
Hello Daniella,

when using an attribute in your link personalization it should be unique. Else you are running in the problem you described below. Therefore I would not recommend the attribute "ID" in this scenario.

To achieve, that your attribute will be unique there are several different approaches you could implement (BAdI, new custom field, HANA view. etc.). Would recommend that you use the functionality of "Subdivided Child Attributes". Please have a look at the following documentation:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MARKETING_CLOUD/13d84c47bb6749a188fd53915c256516/3939940fad6e4ac296c353ce3e30fa97.html

You create basically a new custom attribute based on your ID-Origin. Then only the BuPa ID is available in your attribute and you can use it in your link. 

What makes me a bit curious about your scenario is that a contact can have multiple BuPa IDs. Is this really the case? In the ID-Origin customizing the ID should be maintained in standard as "OnePerContact". This means that for each contact only one BuPa ID can exist. Can you please double check?

Thanks & Br,
Jan

entry.title: Re: Encountered an error when attempting to retrieve data...
entry.content:
Hi @manish19 ,

The "infinite recursion" error might occur due to bi-directional relationships in your entity classes.
For eg: if two entities reference each other, then the serialization process can run into an infinite loop.

Some approaches that you can try here are as follows:
1. You can use is try with @JsonIgnore annotation in one side of the entity to prevent it from being serialized.
2. You can leverage the usage of DTO conversion, instead of directly serializing the entities you can convert them into the DTOs (Data Trasnfer Object) that would only contain the required fields.
3. @JsonManagedReference on the parent entity side and @JsonBackReference on the child entity side may also handle bidirectional relationships.

Hope this helps! Have a great day ahead!

Thanks,
Anjali Pathak

entry.title: Two-Year Race To Implement IT Transformation Across 40 Countries: The Story of Accelleron
entry.content:
In the complex world of business, undergoing a company-wide digital transformation can be a daunting task. Depending on its scope and impact, such a project requires careful planning, efficient implementation, and versatile thinking to mitigate potential risks and maximize outcomes. Accelleron, a global leader in turbocharging, fuel injection and digital solutions, has embarked on such a journey, implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition across 40 countries. This blog explores their challenging yet rewarding journey, outlining the role localization played in their success.

Accelleron: IT Transformation to a Unified Global Service Organization

Accelleron is at the forefront of large turbocharger manufacturing and servicing, primarily serving the marine and power plant sectors. Originally the turbocharging division of ABB Limited, Accelleron became an independent company when a deal to spin out Accelleron from parent ABB Limited was approved, a transition service agreement (TSA) of 24 months was put in place to complete the divestiture. This separation was an opportunity to transform their IT landscape, allowing them to streamline processes and optimize operations.

The main challenge was consolidating their round-the-clock service business, previously managed across more than 40 countries with 21 different ERP systems. This fragmented IT infrastructure posed challenges to the efficiency and coordination of their already established unified global service organization, impacting their ability to operate seamlessly across global platforms. By adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Accelleron successfully streamlined these disparate ERP systems into a single platform with ready-to-run best practices for over 40 countries.

Opportunities on the Road to Transformation

Implementing a global ERP application is a significant and exciting undertaking – particularly under a tight deadline and the goal to adhere to the regulations in over 40 countries. The ability to cover the majority of these countries with SAP delivered local version and the flexibility of custom solutions for the remaining countries proven to be invaluable.

Transitioning to a global platform provided a chance to drive a positive cultural shift within the organization. Accelleron ensured a smooth adoption by engaging their entire organization and securing strong executive sponsorship in this transformative journey.

Since Accelleron's IT ecosystem extends beyond their ERP software, they leveraged the capabilities of public cloud APIs to integrate with existing Customer Relationship Management (CRM), Human Resources (HR), and Finance applications and thus ensured end-to-end processes across applications.

Streamlining Global IT Operations with Effective Localization Strategies

Accelleron's decision to implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has significantly streamlined operations, leveraging effective localization strategies to enhance their already robust global operational capabilities. This targeted approach has optimized the company's response strategy, ensuring continued efficiency in delivering expedited maintenance support and spare parts anywhere in the world within a 48-hour timeframe - a capability they have successfully maintained for years. The cornerstone of their success in streamlining global IT operations was the emphasis on effective localization strategies tailored to the needs of over 40 countries.

By prioritizing compliance with local regulations and addressing country-specific business needs enabled by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Accelleron achieved several key benefits:

  • Strategic Global and Local Integration: The strategic decision to leverage SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition facilitated the harmonization of global processes with local adaptations, enabling Accelleron to balance coherence and flexibility, streamline operations, and improve onboarding by reducing errors and confusion.
  • Compliance-Driven Scalability: The localization capabilities ensured that Accelleron could adapt swiftly to local regulatory requirements, maintaining compliance while unlocking broader business opportunities through scalable and flexible operational frameworks.
  • Rapid Implementation Efficiency: Leveraging localization capabilities, Accelleron achieved swift implementation across 40 countries, minimizing business disruption and maximizing efficiency, showcasing how targeted solutions can expedite complex IT transformations.

These achievements highlight Accelleron's strategic approach to optimizing global operations by leveraging SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with effective localization, demonstrating how it drives both efficiency and growth.

Keeping Pace with Regulatory Changes across the Globe

In a rapidly changing regulatory landscape, implementing a robust ERP is just the beginning. Accelleron understands that staying compliant with evolving requirements and maintaining an adaptive system is crucial for future-readiness. Gathering timely and accurate information is key to this endeavor. Accelleron’s strategy involves multiple sources, with business colleagues and strategic partnerships playing a vital role in collecting insights. Additionally, they leverage SAP's regulatory change manager to get timely information on upcoming regulatory changes that affect SAP products, to stay ahead of compliance needs.

Prompt analysis of new regulatory mandates allows Accelleron to align swiftly with SAP’s roadmap. In countries with SAP-delivered local versions, customers such as Accelleron rely on SAP to keep these updated.

A particular focus area is e-invoicing, where regulatory demands vary significantly by country and transaction type. For instance, European countries are progressively adopting e-invoicing, causing constant updates to SAP software.

Summary

Accelleron's remarkable journey showcases the transformative power of a well-orchestrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation, particularly in a global context. By embracing standardization, prioritizing localization, and fostering strong partnerships, Accelleron successfully navigated the complexities of a large-scale ERP rollout in a notably short time. Their story serves as a valuable example for other organizations looking to achieve similar IT transformations on a global scale.

Learn about Localization Today: 

Accelleron shares their experience with localized SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Accelleron: Turbocharging a divestiture and resolving data fragmentation challenges with advanced ERP in the cloud

Localization Capabilities on sap.com 

Accelerate Global Business Readiness with Localized SAP S/4HANA - SAPinsider

SAP Community for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

SAP Community for Localization 

Regulatory Change Manager: Your Solution to Navigate Regulatory Changes with Ease

entry.title: SAP Ukraine у цифровому просторі
entry.content:
Попри тимчасове закриття київського офісу з міркувань безпеки, SAP - активно використовує цифрові інструменти для ефективної співпраці, швидкої комунікації та поширення ідей.

Цифрове середовище стало  новою робочою реальністю - ми змогли адаптуватися до нових викликів і сформувати цифровий простір, де продовжуємо розвивати проєкти, ділитися досвідом, та залишатися на зв’язку з глобальною мережею SAP.

Завдяки цьому - ми тут, ми працюємо, ми підтримуємо одне одного -  навіть коли перебуваємо у різних куточках України та світу.

Саме у цій спільноті ми творимо нову історію, де професіоналізм і людська підтримка нерозривно поєднані в одне ціле.

Це середовище нашого професійного розвитку, культура співпраці та точка об’єднання всієї екосистеми SAP в Україні і за її межами.

Команда #SAPUkraine

А як відбувається ваша співпраця з SAP спеціалістами та командами? Поділіться фото зустрічей з SAP у коментарях!
