entry.title: Re: Carrier integrations to SAP TM - What to choose?
entry.content:
Hello, 

I can relate to what´s written here and specifically on the topic of effort estimations the endeavour to get those transparent upfront appears to be often times like rolling dices because of the manifold spectrum of scenarios; but one thing is clear: taking a shipper POV ultimately the intgration success stands and falls with both the business and technical caliber of expertise the carrier can provide (yes and time too).

Take a BNL Freight Colaboration use case or other, the moment you want to send real physical payloads (JSON, EDIFACT, ANSI X12) down to and make sure it lands in the carrier backend successfully you always have per interface a mid-size 2 digit PD number for functional scoping + system configuration + payload robust testing UAT + buffer. Whatever the number in the end might be its effort ratio split between shipper and carrier is empirically a rough 30/70% (in favor of the shipper).

Such figures are yet heuristics though but can become a dangerous sin in case modern software projects a.) ignore that building and testing does not magically happen by itself and b.) that this multiplies with the number of carriers.

entry.title: Finance North Star Architecture
entry.content:
Changing Requirements of Finance Require Evolved Finance Software

The role of finance leaders, including CFOs and controllers, is evolving from traditional scorekeeping to strategic financial stewardship. They now focus on aligning capital and business strategy, positioning finance teams as integral partners in shaping the company's future. With this expanded role come new objectives (manage uncertainty, navigate transformation, and enable compliance and sustainability), emphasizing the need for adaptable financial management, as well as collaboration within the whole finance organization and beyond.  

To achieve those goals in finance, SAP Business Suite has got you covered.   

SAP Business Suite offers a comprehensive and robust combination of business applications, automated processes, business data management, and artificial intelligence features that cater to diverse organizational needs. This suite includes a variety of integrated solutions designed to optimize enterprise resource planning, customer relationship management, supply chain management, human capital management, and financial management. By automating processes and integrating data, SAP's Business Suite enhances operational efficiency, reduces manual inputs and minimizes errors. 

Moreover, business data is meticulously organized and analyzed to provide actionable insights that drive informed decision-making. The integration of advanced AI capabilities further empowers finance teams, enabling predictive analytics, real-time forecasting, and adaptive learning across business operations. This combination ensures that businesses are equipped with innovative tools that not only streamline their existing workflows but also position them to anticipate future challenges and opportunities in a dynamic environment. 

The journey towards SAP Business Suite is customer-specific, depending on the individual starting point. To address this, we introduce the SAP Reference Architecture.  

 

What is the Finance North Star Architecture? 

The SAP Reference Architecture consists of the SAP Reference Business Architecture (RBA) and SAP Reference Solution Architecture (RSA). The Finance North Star Architecture is the part of RSA that is related to finance. It is designed to showcase how organizations can navigate their financial transformation journey to SAP Business Suite. This architecture provides a detailed blueprint for integrating SAP's advanced financial solutions, ensuring alignment with business objectives and efficient management across all finance functions. SAP Reference Architecture is documented in SAP LeanIX, a leading enterprise architecture management solution, which houses comprehensive information and resources for planning and executing transformation projects. 

Within the Finance North Star Architecture, organizations can explore a structured, cloud-centric approach, adopting strategic capabilities that align SAP's Cloud ERP solutions with the evolving demands of modern finance. It highlights SAP's suite of Software-as-a-Service applications which comprise cutting-edge innovations in financial planning, analysis, accounting, and compliance. Moreover, SAP's Reference Architecture in general offers industry-specific templates and best practices, enabling companies to customize their approaches according to their individual needs and operational contexts, thereby optimizing their workflows and achieving strategic goals. The Finance North Star Architecture showcases the architecture that results from adopting the whole SAP portfolio in the finance area.  

 

How Can the Finance North Star Architecture Help You to Transform Your Finance to SAP Business Suite? 

The Finance North Star Architecture serves as a guide for financial transformation, facilitating the transition to SAP Business Suite for both new and existing customers. It employs a North Star approach, directing organizations in structuring their finance operations in alignment with SAP’s suite of solutions and innovation strategy. For new customers or those adopting a greenfield approach, the architecture provides a streamlined pathway, utilizing templates and resources from the SAP LeanIX Reference Catalog. 

As a first step, users can explore business capabilities in the SAP LeanIX Reference Catalog, offering suggested SAP solutions. Utilizing road map planning functionality, businesses can collect and track essential data for business process transformation initiatives, translate those initiatives into IT initiatives and landscape models, visualize process transformation roadmaps, and evaluate execution against their process transformation models. This way, businesses can seamlessly prepare and execute transformations, whether implementing new applications or adapting existing ones.  

Apart from SAP LeanIX, the Finance North Star Architecture also leverages SAP Signavio: within SAP Signavio Process Explorer, teams can see insights regarding SAP Reference Processes and transformation strategies. 

Involving Key Users of specific finance areas enables organizations to take transformation discussions to the affected roles and decision makers beyond the core IT team. Business language is used rather than technical keys to facilitate discussions around objectives and capabilities.   
Existing AI capabilities are mapped to our solutions, allowing organizations to quickly identify where SAP Business AI can support in optimizing finance.  

*Disclaimer: please note that the diagrams depicted here are not available out of the box. They result from adopting the whole SAP portfolio in finance.  

For existing customers, the architecture supports a hybrid approach that blends private cloud and public cloud solutions, facilitating scalability, flexibility, and access to the latest innovations. By utilizing tools such as SAP Business Technology Platform add-ons and leveraging strategic partnerships like the one with BlackLine for automating account substantiation, companies can extend their financial capabilities while maintaining clean core adherence for optimized business outcomes. The Finance North Star Architecture empowers teams to detail actionable changes, track value realization, and achieve a future-ready finance department seamlessly.  

*Disclaimer: please note that the diagrams depicted here are not available out of the box. They result from adopting the whole SAP portfolio in finance. 

 

Benefits of Utilizing the Finance North Star Architecture 

The Finance North Star Architecture demonstrates the potential transformation of your finance functions by implementing the complete suite of SAP solutions. And it lets SAP customers know which the go-to solutions are – where the focus of innovation will be in the coming years. 

It supports organizations in overcoming uncertainty, enhancing efficiency, and fostering innovation. It empowers clean core adherence by offering a hybrid architectural approach that dismantles outdated silos, facilitating smoother integration of new technologies. SAP's Software-as-a-Service solutions, such as SAP Business Technology Platform add-ons and digital banking extensions, offer immediate access to innovations and scalable solutions that bolster agility and compliance. The Finance North Star Architecture empowers finance teams to chart their transformation journey, ensuring faster rollouts, reduced risk, and measurable value realization. With SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio, companies can manage transformations effectively, tracking the impact and benefits of their strategic initiatives, ultimately leading to a future-ready finance team. 

More Information

Finance North Star Architecture Demo Video

Business Capabilities in the Reference Catalog - SAP LeanIX User Docs

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 1 - Concepts
entry.content:
This is a blog series for the topic of Sustainability as SAP Customers need guidance to define their target sustainability application architecture and roadmap to achieve their sustainability goals using Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF) methodology. :
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 1 - Concepts
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 2 - Artifacts
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 3 - Products and Services

Please refer to S/4HANA SQUARE ONE for my other blogs

 

SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS

Sustainability requirements focus on the call to action from the 2030 agenda for Sustainable Development which was adopted by United Nations in 2015 as per the Paris Agreement with 17 sustainable Development Goals (SDG’s). Our main focus is on 8 goals with black border. The SDG are achieved by prioritizing Business Capabilities which are mapped to best-fit applications to define the future target application architecture roadmap. For more information see here

The regulations that are in force for Sustainability are:
CBAM - Cross Border Adjustment Mechanism (e.g. hydrogen, Cement, Iron, Steel, Fertilizer, Aluminum)
CSRD - Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
EPR - Extended Producer Responsibility
ESRS - European Sustainability Reporting Standard
EUDR - European Deforestation (e.g. soy, beef, palm oil, wood, cocoa, coffee, rubber, and derivate)
ISSB - International Sustainability Standards Board

 

SUSTAINABILITY AT SAP - TIMELINE

In May 2006 SAP released Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) reporting compliance capabilities for 1st generation EPR policies as a licensed product for SAP R/3 4.6C.
In 2012, with the adoption of Human Rights Commitment Statement, publishing the first integrator report, long before it was legally required or even mainstream. We were the first software company to report on our social and environmental performance alongside our financial performance.
As of 2014 SAP REA (SAP Recycle Administration) was included in ECC core and will reach end of maintenance in 2030 (ECC), and 2040 (S/4HANA). REA services team has transitioned to support SAP RDP and other solutions.
Since 2014, all our data centers and facilities were powered with 100% renewable electricity. While we set our first carbon emission reduction targets in 2009, in 2017, our science-based target CO2 reduction for 2050 was approved.
In 2021 SAP Responsible Design and Production was released as a SaaS solution that provides reporting logic and reports as a service for EPR, plastic tax, and NGO reporting, plus enhanced analytics.
In 2022, we launched SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises. This includes both new products, like the SAP Sustainability Control Tower, and a heritage of longstanding sustainability solutions like SAP Environment, Health, and Safety Management.
In 2023, SAP made the strategic decision to add our leading solutions (SAP Sustainability Control Tower and SAP Sustainability Footprint Management) to the RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, premium plus bundle, exclusively embedding sustainability in our cloud ERP transformation value proposition.
In 2024 SAP Sustainability Data Exchange was added to the SAP Business Network solution area, SAP Green Token was added to the supply chain management solution area and SAP Green Ledger which brings carbon management functionality to the office of the CFO.

 

CARBON EMMISSION SCOPES

Carbon Emissions Scope 1: Direct Emissions from sources that are owned or controlled by the organization (Company owned vehicles, emissions from Stationary Combustion, Mobile Combustion, Fugitive Emissions, and Process Emissions from manufacturing)
Carbon Emissions Scope 2: Indirect Emissions outside of a company’s direct control but are a result of energy consumption from purchased electricity, steam, heat and cooling.
Carbon Emissions Scope 3: Indirect Emissions that are not directly owned and controlled by the organization  including emissions from the supply chain, business travel, employee commuting, waste disposal, and product life cycles. These emissions involve many stakeholders, so collecting accurate data makes measuring and assessing these emissions difficult, and  there is no clarity on who exactly is accountable for reducing.

 

LEARNING HUB

*ROA = Record of Achievement
SAP Solutions for Sustainability (1 min)
Discovering SAP Sustainability Solutions (3.5hrs) 
Positioning the SAP Sustainability Portfolio (4hrs) (ROA)
SAP Certified Associate - Positioning SAP Sustainability Solution as part of Business Suite  - C_BCSSS_2502

EHS: Discovering SAP Environment, Health, and Safety Management (7hrs) (ROA)
EHS-EM: Discovering SAP EHS Environment Management (3hrs) (ROA)
GrL: Discovering Sustainability for SAP Finance (45mins)
PC: Exploring SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance 2022 (16hrs)
PC: Implementing Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2hrs) (ROA)
RDP: Administering and Using Responsible Design and Production for the Circular Economy  (16hrs) (ROA)
SCT: Administering Sustainability Control Tower (2hrs) (ROA)
SCT: Reporting and Steering Sustainability in your Business using SAP Sustainability Control Tower (4hrs) (ROA)
SDX: Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (1hr) (ROA)
SFM: Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration (2hrs) (ROA)
SFM: Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configurations (3hrs)

PC: Product Compliance in a Nutshell (1.5hrs) (OPENSAP)
RDP: Design for a Circular Economy (3hrs) (OPENSAP)
SCT: Getting Started with ESG Steering and Reporting (4hrs) (OPENSAP)
SFM: Climate Action with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (3.5hrs) (OPENSAP)
SFM: Taking Action on Climate Change with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (3.5hrs) (OPENSAP)

 

Further Information

For further information or any questions & feedback, please refer to:

  • Sustainability on SAP.com
  • SAP.help – Sustainability Solutions
  • SAP Road Map Explorer Sustainability
  • SAP Customer Reference platform Sustainability
  • SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises – Integration Aspects
  • SAP Community - Sustainability
  • Sustainability Adoption Support DL: Sustainability_Adoption_Support@global.corp.sap

Thank you,

Mahesh Sardesai
SAP Canada

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 2 - Artifacts
entry.content:
This is a blog series for the topic of Sustainability as SAP Customers need guidance to define their target sustainability application architecture and roadmap to achieve their sustainability goals:
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 1 - Concepts
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 2 - Artifacts
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 3 - Products and Services

Please refer to S/4HANA SQUARE ONE for my other blogs

This section shows approach to ensure that sustainability strategy is reflected in the Enterprise Architecture to achieve sustainability goals. The organization may already have some traditional sustainability applications in the EH&S area (Environmental Health & Safety) like Product Compliance or EHS-incident management currently running in the SAP ECC solution. They may be interested in the Architecture options and product roadmap for the next generation SAP sustainability applications such as SAP Sustainability Control Tower, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Responsible Design for Production.
The Enterprise Architect will develop the Target Architecture Roadmap based on sustainability priorities as shown below. Our approach follows the Enterprise Architecture methodology in five key phases (1) to (5) as shown. Throughout these phases, sustainability-specific content is incorporated to ensure it is effectively leveraged during architecture planning and execution.

Before we go into Business Strategy we will first explore SAP’s Reference Business Architecture.

 

Reference Business Architecture Content for SAP Sustainability

Reference Architecture Content for Sustainability Management is found as below: Please refer url:
SAP Signavio Process Explorer – Capability Map

 

Sustainability – Cloud Solution Component Map

The Business Capabilities for Sustainability are available in below areas:

  • Sustainability Management
  • R&D Engineering => Product Compliance Management
  • Finance => Accounting and Financial Close => Sustainability Accounting

The 8 applications mapped to these business capabilities are shown in graphic above, highlighted in the green box are further described in detail in the Product Portfolio section.

 

Sustainability Business Capability Map:

Please find below the list of Business Capabilities that are available in LeanIX. You will have to import the required Business Capabilities into LeanIX Inventory.

There are three key business capabilities under R&D Engineering ⇒ Product Compliance Management:

And under Finance => Accounting and Financial Close the Business Capability of Sustainability Accounting as shown below:

Besides the 7 leading L1 Sustainability solutions (in dark blue boxes below), there are several supporting L2 solutions (in grey boxes below).
From a Finance & Spend perspective – or the CFO persona, the following capabilities can be addressed:  

From a Supply Chain Management perspective, the following capabilities can be addressed: 

 

Business Model Canvas

The Sustainable Business Model Canvas is a one page representation of a business model that is used to describe how an organization creates, delivers, and captures value.
It provides a shared perspective and understanding of an organization's business models. It enables informed discussion among business executives on business issues and necessary change. It fosters a design-oriented approach to identify what a business can do differently to maximize value exchanges between market participants. It enables collaboration and co-creation of current-state and future state business model options focusing on the key revenue and cost components supporting an agreed value proposition for a particular customer segment.
Below is the key template for the Sustainability Business Model Canvas, which you can use to document your perspective. Please zoom into the document as needed for better readability.

Source for the template is https://www.strategyzer.com/

 

Business Strategy Map:

We want to first understand what the customers tells us about their strategy, then we use our reference content to come up with this strategy map.
The example in the graphic shows the linkage between strategic priorities and drivers, such as “Operational Compliance,” and their corresponding goals or outcomes, like “demonstrate responsibility as a caring company to attract top talent.” These are then connected to customer objectives or SAP Value Drivers, such as “create a workplace free from fatalities and injuries.” Finally, we link these value drivers to specific business capabilities, such as EHS Incident Management. These linkages can either be part of our reference content or created based on the customer engagement.
At the end of the process, we link this strategy map to business capabilities, providing valuable input for our architecture planning. With this approach, we can identify the core business capabilities that are critical to the customer’s sustainability strategy. From there, we can move forward and develop the target architecture. 

Strategic Decision Paths:
Please find the blogs on strategic decision paths as follows:
Strategic Decision Paths for Managing Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculations (SCT, SFM, EHS-EM)
Strategic Decision Paths for Managing Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and Plastic Taxes
Strategic Decision Paths for Managing Hazardous Substances: SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Migration
Product Compliance: Transition to SAP S/4HANA

 

Sustainability Capability Heatmap L1-L2 Capabilities

Business capability heatmapping includes reviewing business capabilities according to SAP EA Framework (SAP Reference Business Architecture and SAP Reference Solution Architecture content) and mapping to customer business objectives. Heatmapping provides you with a solid decision-making foundation for future investment planning and priorities.
First we arrive at the Business Capabilities in scope as shown below:

 

Business Capability Prioritization

Below graph shows business capability prioritization. As you can see, the traditional capabilities like Dangerous Goods Management or Hazardous Substance Management the current fulfillment of Target Capability is high because customer has been using this from many years so it shows on the right hand side. Also the customer may be using this to report back when shipping goods.
The newer solution Business Criticality is high but the current fulfillment of Target Capability is low for Circular Economy, Sustainability Footprint Management, EHS Incident Management.

 

Applications Architecture Roadmap

This is a sample Sustainability Target Architecture Roadmap that has been created after conducting workshop sessions as per business domain, sketching target application architecture, and focusing on key sustainability business areas. This is a multiyear, strategic roadmap that focuses on sustainability which you can leverage as an example.

 

Target Applications Architecture Diagram

Please find below the Target Application Architecture diagram.

 

Enterprise Architecture Framework Services

Architecture Framework Services uses a modular service approach of North Star Architecture using EA tooling and Reference Architecture content.  Services can be sourced from SAP, other external providers, or provided by the organization performing the practice. They can include enablement of different EA target roles in customers' and partners' organizations provided. This support can be run jointly by customer and SAP teams or by only one of the parties.
The North Star Architecture service patterns are predefined flows of selected modules with a proposed level of depts that helps solve customer problems.

Generally for transformation into sustainable enterprise, the key architecture modules in the North Star Service are as follows. You may add more modules as required depending on the customer requirements.

  • Strategy Mapping – This module assesses the sustainability strategic objectives of the organization and how they relate to the goals and value drivers of the organization.
  • Business Capability – This module focuses on heatmapping the business capabilities that are in scope and out of scope and also focuses on pain points with existing applications and opportunities to move to new SAP applications.
  • Application Architecture – This module focuses on designing the target application architecture for SAP sustainability applications, along with the high-level solution components map to provide visibility into the business capabilities mapping to solution capabilities of SAP applications.
  • Initiatives and Roadmap – This module helps to determine a set of transformation initiatives for sustainability; lists the major roadmap clusters, phases, and dependencies; and then sequences them on a relative timescale to create the sustainability roadmap.

 

Further Information

For further information or any questions & feedback, please refer to:

  • Sustainability on SAP.com
  • SAP.help – Sustainability Solutions
  • SAP Community - Sustainability
  • SAP Road Map Explorer Sustainability
  • SAP Customer Reference platform Sustainability
  • SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises – Integration Aspects
  • Sustainability Adoption Support DL: Sustainability_Adoption_Support@global.corp.sap

A big shout out to the Peer Reviewers @JansiMurugesan  @LlianaPons

Thank you,

Mahesh Sardesai
SAP Canada

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 3 - Products and Services
entry.content:
This is a blog series for the topic of Sustainability as SAP Customers need guidance to define their target sustainability application architecture and roadmap to achieve their sustainability goals:
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 1 - Concepts
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 2 - Artifacts
SAP Sustainability Architecture - Part 3 - Products and Services

Please refer to S/4HANA SQUARE ONE for my other blogs

Sustainability is a key driver of business success and strategic differentiation in the modern economy. To meet changing market demand faster and cheaper and increase resilience for future challenges, sustainability needs to be at the core of every business process.
Sustainability is thus a broad topic that covers a variety of themes and use cases. To address this, SAP offers a modular portfolio that cover most relevant sustainability use cases & capabilities, and can be summarized across four themes:

  • ESG Reporting & Management
  • Carbon Management
  • Material Transition
  • Operational Compliance

SAP Sustainability’s approach is ERP-centric, with modular, integrated, connected and AI-enabled solutions that leverage and extend existing enterprise capabilities.


The layered modular solution portfolio is built on a common foundation (S/4HANA, BTP) and harmonized data model, ensuring consistency, transparency, and interoperability across our customers’ organizations.
Below is an overview of the SAP Sustainability portfolio (L1 public cloud, as of February 2025; for the latest, please always refer to the Sustainability GTM Workzone). Please find details on each of the products as shown in the diagram:

 

1) SAP Sustainability Data Exchange

The SAP Sustainability Data Exchange integrates seamlessly with our Business Network, ensuring supply chain transparency, compliance, and collaboration at scale.
SAP Sustainability Data Exchange engages with companies’ suppliers and requests and receives accurate footprint calculation from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is a new solution and uses the carbon data interoperability standards established by the Partnership for Carbon Transparency (PACT), hosted by the World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD).
For the latest information and assets, see here.
INTEGRATION: As shown below SDX is a cloud-based solution that facilitates easy sharing of scope 3 emission data between business partners in a standardized way using SAP Business Network, and industry networks, such as Catena-X, Factory-X as data collaboration channels. It transfers Supplier data to SFM, and shares the SFM data with Customer.

 

2) SAP Sustainability Control Tower

SAP Sustainability Control Tower helps to maintain the agility to report with trusted, audit-ready metrics, monitor and steer Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) targets as well as report on sustainability performance beyond carbon (for example covering diversity & equality KPIs). Through the integration with SAP Sustainability Control Tower, corporate footprints that are calculated with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management can now also be integrated into the overall sustainability reporting and used for company-wide holistic ESG reporting and steering.
For the latest information and assets, see here and for strategic decision path see here.
INTEGRATION: From SCT the data is transferred using Outbound API Interface, generally an OData interface for data replication with OAuth 2.0 client credentials. The Inbound Interface is used for Local CSV file upload to commodity pricing engine (CPE) component, Central replication service using SAP BTP destinations and authentication features, SAP S/4HANA integration via SAP BTP destinations and cloud connector for on-premises solutions and SAP Datasphere integration through the ODATA interface. Also uses Inbound API as ODATA interface.

 

3) SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management helps companies to calculate their corporate and product carbon footprints across GHG Scope 1, 2, and 3 and embed the results into existing business processes to drive decarbonization. It assesses corporate carbon footprint (CCF) across all GHG-Protocol categories and product carbon footprints (PCF) that consider materials, transportation, and production.
For the latest information and assets, see here and for strategic decision path see here..
INTEGRATION: The data is extracted from SAP S/4HANA End-to-End process, SAP EHS Management, Environment management, SAP Sustainability Data Exchange and Third-party providers and imported into SAP Green Ledger, SAP Sustainability Control Tower Partner's SAP Sustainability Data Exchange or SAP S/4HANA system.

 

4) SAP Green Ledger

SAP Green Ledger allocates carbon emissions to specific economic activities and transactions captured by ERP solutions from SAP. This innovation empowers organizations to accurately account for, analyze and report carbon footprints across products, services, and organizational units.
The figure below shows carbon accounting foundation based on trusted financial processes/structures. Here we treat Carbon like Money like double entry book-keeping.
For the latest information and assets, see here.

 

5) SAP Green Token

SAP Green Token is a software-as-a-service (SaaS) cloud solution that provides traceability and transparency for raw materials in the context of ESG (Environment, Social, and Governance) attributes across the supply chain. Leveraging the innovative principles of Tokenization, Blockchain, and Chain of Custody Models; with segregation or mass balancing and tokenization for trust and compliance with recognized standards. The solution enables accurate raw material accounting, proving material provenance, and transitioning to sustainable material usage.
SAP Green Token captures sustainability attributes and documentation at inbound. Conversions are captured and this data and proof is maintained throughout complex supply chain processes and productions. As a result, full visibility on your raw materials and proof of sustainability is created.
For the latest information and assets, see here.

 

6) SAP Responsible Design and Production

SAP Responsible Design and Production helps design products sustainably and build a regenerative business in a circular economy, e.g. by providing the ability to calculate and report Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) fees and plastic taxes and by giving decision makers a lifecycle view into costs and planetary impact of design choices. It helps producers manage their Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations and plastic taxes so they can control and eliminate the costs of the downstream waste system and make design changes to eliminate waste.
For the latest information and assets, see here.
INTEGRATION: Most data for EPR is coming from Material Management, Sales & Distribution, and Production Planning, as well as packaging information out of SAP PLM or 3rd-party systems. There is option for Data Mapping: Data collection & data mapping using ABAP code. In the middle part, there is SAP Integration Suita and Connectivity Service option for data mapping ERP and packaging data to the RDP unified data model on the right.

 

7) SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Environment, Health, and Safety Management

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Environmental, Health, and Safety Management (EHS) supports your organization in managing business processes related to the safety of the environment and the health and safety of people. It covers several use cases. In a sustainability context, the most relevant modules are:

  1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS Environmental Management (EHS EM) ensures companies stay compliant with emissions and permit related environmental regulations. EHS EM encompasses all aspects of environmental compliance along with management of an organization's impact on the environment, including air and water pollution, GHG emission inventory, and waste management.
    For the latest information and assets, see here.
  2. SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS workplace safety tracks safety compliance and incidents in the cloud. With advanced and automated safety software that supports hazard identification and risk mitigation, customers can promote a more proactive safety culture to keep workers safe and productive.
    For the latest information and assets, see here.

 

8] SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Product Compliance

Operational Sustainability with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Product Compliance (PC) supports product safety and compliance throughout the product lifecycle and integrates in all business processes. Use the solution to protect revenue and brand, reduce risk and compliance cost, and streamline compliance processes.
CLASSIC v/s NEW: Classic capabilities of PC are available in S/4HANA on-premise and private cloud and will not be enhanced (migrate by 2031). New PC capabilities are available in SAP S/4HANA on-premise, public and private cloud - new CDS-based business process model, SAP Fiori GUI technology to develop more powerful analytics, enhanced business process integration into product master, sales delivery, procurement and transportation, see here.
For the latest information and assets, see here.

AI for Sustainability

SAP Sustainability portfolio & AI

The SAP Sustainability portfolio leverages artificial intelligence (AI) to drive significant advancements in sustainability across various business functions. The integration of AI into SAP Sustainability solutions enables organizations to enhance their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) performance through data-driven insights, automation, and predictive analytics.

AI addresses several key challenges in sustainability, including the fragmentation and siloing of sustainability-related data, the complexity and manual nature of sustainability processes, and the difficulty in obtaining accurate and actionable insights. By unifying and enriching data from various sources, AI ensures that organizations have access to accurate and actionable information. It also automates complex tasks, reducing human error and freeing up resources for strategic activities. Additionally, AI provides tailored and predictive insights, enabling informed decisions that optimize both environmental and business outcomes.
Today, AI is already being utilized in various innovative ways within SAP's sustainability portfolio to address key challenges and drive efficiency. Here are some of the most prominent AI use cases:

  1. Emission Factor Mapping in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: AI assists in mapping emission factors to purchased products, significantly reducing manual effort and time and increasing accuracy in calculating product and corporate carbon footprints by up to 80%.
  2. ESG Report Generation in SAP Sustainability Control Tower: AI generates comprehensive ESG reports by automatically collecting relevant metrics, creating visual graphs, and drafting reports based on best-practice templates. This can lead to up to 98% reduction in time needed to collect ESG metrics and 80% reduction in time needed to create and draft reports, allowing sustainability teams to focus on execution and management.
  3. Declaration Image Analysis in SAP Green Token: AI autonomously reviews and extracts information from supplier sustainability declarations, regardless of their format, and posts the data to SAP Green Token.

Further AI use cases are currently in beta or being planned for later in 2025. Below you can find an overview as of May 2025:

For the latest information and assets, see here.

Ethical and sustainable AI

SAP is committed to responsible AI development, ensuring that AI solutions are built with security, privacy, compliance, ethics, and accuracy in mind. Some of the key ethical considerations are:

  • Bias and Fairness: AI must be designed to prevent the encoding of patterns of marginalization, inequality and discrimination.
  • Transparency and Explainability: SAP implements mechanisms to provide clear explanations for the reasoning behind AI-generated outputs, making the model's behavior understandable to users and stakeholders.
  • Privacy and Data Security: SAP ensures that AI solutions comply with data privacy regulations and protect sensitive information from unauthorized access.
  • Human Agency and Oversight: SAP maintains human control over critical decisions and interventions to avoid unintended consequences and ensure ethical use of AI.
  • Continuous Monitoring: SAP continuously monitors AI performance and outputs to detect any deviations from ethical and responsible behavior.

SAP's AI ethics policy, governance model, and sustainable development guidelines ensure that AI use cases have a positive impact on people and the planet.
For the latest information and assets, see here.

 

SAP Sustainability with RISE / GROW

In the second quarter of 2023, SAP launched the Case for Cloud initiative. “Case for Cloud” encompasses all programs and innovation designed to make SAP’s cloud solutions more attractive to our on-premise installed base customers.
IMPORTANT: Strategic Cloud innovations are only being offered in the context of RISE or GROW with SAP.
Two of the Sustainability solutions, namely SAP Sustainability Control Tower and SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, as well as the Finance solution SAP Green Ledger, are considered strategic cloud innovations and can thus only be sold to customers with an existing RISE or GROW contract (often referred to as ‘RISE-/GROW pre-rec).
The other Sustainability solutions do not yet have a RISE-/GROW pre-rec, which may change in the future. To stay informed, please refer to the Case for Cloud FAQ.

As of May 2025, there are two existing suite bundles that include Sustainability solutions:

  1. RISE with SAP, Sustainability essentials package with SAP Sustainability Control Tower, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, and SAP Sustainability Data Exchange.
  2. SAP Supply Chain Premium (‘SCM Premium’) with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS environment management, SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS workplace safety, SAP S/4HANA Cloud for product compliance, SAP Responsible Design and Production, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Sustainability Data Exchange.

As bundles may evolve, please refer to the latest information regarding commercials to the Sustainability GTM Workzone.

 

SAP Sustainability Service Portfolio

The Sustainability Service Portfolio offers a range of services to meet our customers where they are at – from kicking of the journey, bridging the gap between strategy and execution, to delivering, running and scaling the sustainable enterprise.  
The portfolio includes advisory, assessment, activation and implementation services:

Sustainability Advisory

The Sustainability Advisory helps customers understand the business opportunities and risks related to sustainability and climate action, and helps companies respond by evaluating impacts and outcomes. This scope option provides our customers a holistic approach to kick-off their transformation towards the Sustainable Enterprise.

Sustainability Enablement

Bridge the gap between strategy (sustainability goals and ambitions) and execution (deriving an implementation plan). Identify data in existing systems and link it to an SAP Sustainability solution in order to achieve setting sustainability ambitions.

Sustainability Adoption

Maximize usage and adoption of SAP Sustainability solutions. Different service offerings available, depending on the customer situation. Supporting the explore phase, building a strong foundation in the test tenant, or supporting our customers until business go-live.

There are also as well as options under SAP Premium Engagement and SAP Preferred Success. The holistic portfolio overview and link to sales channels can be accessed here.
NOTE: Sustainability will also be part of the SAP Services evolution program and initiative (expected launch in 2026).

 

Further Information

For further information or any questions & feedback, please refer to:

  • Sustainability on SAP.com
  • SAP.help – Sustainability Solutions
  • SAP Community - Sustainability
  • SAP Road Map Explorer Sustainability
  • SAP Customer Reference platform Sustainability
  • SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises – Integration Aspects
  • Sustainability Adoption Support DL: Sustainability_Adoption_Support@global.corp.sap

 

Thank you,

Mahesh Sardesai
SAP Canada

entry.title: If This, Then That: Making Sense of Identity Provisioning Conditions
entry.content:
How do you set conditions in Identity Provisioning transformations to get the results you need? It’s not a million-dollar question, but for those in need, who knows, it might be close.

Conditions let you filter data by defining criteria that must be met. In other words, something must (or must not) exist for something else to happen.

For example: "condition": "$.userName =~ /.*JohnSmith.*/"

This condition checks whether the userName matches the regular expression /.*JohnSmith.*/. If the value of userName contains JohnSmith, the condition evaluates to true, and the user is provisioned. All other users are skipped.

Conditions are defined following the JSONPath specification and can be applied in both read and write transformations, using either the JSON editor or the Graphical editor, depending on your preference.

JSON Editor:

{

    "user": {

        "condition": "$.userName =~ /.*JohnSmith*/",

 

Graphical Editor:

 

Simple examples like this are often used for testing. In real-world scenarios, customers rarely provision just a single user. Instead, they need more specific and complex cases, often involving filtering users based on group assignments.

If you’ve ever found yourself stuck writing a condition, this new resource is for you: Transformation Conditions.

We’ve added a collection of practical condition examples to the Identity Provisioning documentation to help you define and apply them more easily.

Your next working condition might be just a scroll away!
